$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Energy repricing risk from Black Sea maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding energy repricing through gas supply stress, with 68% pressure and 73% state confirmation. For now, the base case stays near 64% over the 30d. Actors absorb the current...
foil.perspective_strategic: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding energy repricing through gas supply stress, with 68% pressure and 73% state confirmation is setting the strategic baseline, and the current 61% probability implies a live but not settled risk path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The current 75% confidence level still leaves room for reversal. If that restraint persists, the forecast can move below the current 61% baseline. Actors prioritize containment and the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 61%.
foil.top_signal: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding energy repricing through gas supply stress, with 68% pressure and 73% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
